title: Freeze on Death with Loss Reason Display
content:
## Overview When any snake dies, the game enters a **death freeze** instead of immediately jumping to the Game Over screen. The board stays visible and frozen, the fatal collision cell is highlighted in amber at its correct isometric depth, a pulsing banner names the loss reason, and the game waits for any key press before advancing to the normal Game Over screen. This applies to all modes: single-player (human or bot), two-player PvP, and bot-vs-bot. Before this feature, `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` were called immediately on death, which called `showScreen('game-over')` and set `display:none` on `#game`, hiding the canvas entirely. The fatal head cell was never drawn because collision was checked before `this.snake.unshift(head)` and `draw()` was never called on the death path. ## Architecture ### New Instance Fields (`src/main.ts`) Four fields added alongside the existing `deathCause` field (~line 154): | Field | Type | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `deathReason` | `'wall' \| 'self' \| 'opponent' \| 'head-to-head' \| 'starved'` | Banner-facing loss reason | | `deathCells` | `Array<{ x: number; y: number; who: 1 \| 2 }>` | Fatal head cell(s) to highlight; empty for starvation | | `awaitingDeathAck` | `boolean` | `true` during the freeze | | `pendingFinalize` | `(() => void) \| null` | Deferred end-game callback | The existing `deathCause: 'collision' | 'starved'` field is **unchanged** — it still drives the Game Over title and winner nuance in `endGame`/`endGameTwoPlayer`. All four new fields are reset in `startNewGame` next to `this.deathCause = 'collision'` (~line 1104), and `hideDeathBanner()` is called beside the pause-hide (~line 1110) so a restart never leaves a stuck banner or stale alarm cell. ### `beginDeathFreeze(finalize: () => void)` (near line 1801) The central method that initiates the freeze: 1. Sets `isGameOver = true`, `awaitingDeathAck = true`, `pendingFinalize = finalize`. 2. Calls `stopAutoRotate()` and clears the game loop interval (same lines `endGame` runs — idempotent when `endGame` re-runs them at finalize time). 3. Hides `#starvation-warning` so it doesn't overlap the death banner. 4. Calls `this.draw()` to render the frozen board with the death cell highlight. 5. Calls `this.showDeathBanner()`. The existing `endGame()` and `endGameTwoPlayer()` functions are passed as the `finalize` callback and run **unchanged** when the freeze ends — all winner resolution, high-score saving, and leaderboard logic is preserved exactly. ### Four Death Sites Rewired (`src/main.ts`) | Site | Location | `deathReason` | `deathCells` | Callback | |---|---|---|---|---| | Single-player collision | ~line 1161 | `'wall'` if OOB, else `'self'` | `[{x:head.x, y:head.y, who:1}]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Single-player starvation | ~line 1183 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGame()` | | Two-player collision | ~line 1231 | `'head-to-head'`, `'wall'`, `'self'`, or `'opponent'` | `head1` if p1Dead, `head2` if p2Dead | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Dead, p2Dead)` | | Two-player starvation | ~line 1275 | `'starved'` | `[]` | `() => this.endGameTwoPlayer(p1Starved, p2Starved)` | For single-player collision, `head` is the pre-`unshift` fatal cell; `this.snake[0]` remains the last valid cell (used for the wall clamp). For two-player collision, per-snake reasons `r1`/`r2` are computed for the banner text. ### Death Cell Rendering in `draw()` — Painter's Algorithm Integration The amber death cell is pushed into the painter's-algorithm object list as a `'death'` type entry **before** sorting, so it is drawn at its correct isometric depth relative to surrounding segments. Array sort stability ensures the marker paints over the segment sharing its cell when depths are equal. The only exception is the **off-grid wall-death sliver**: because an OOB coordinate has no meaningful depth position in the scene, it is still drawn on top afterward as a special case — a sliver that pokes past the border edge as an "off the edge" cue. **Bounds handling** branches on the grid rectangle (`x < 0 || x >= gridSize || y < 0 || y >= gridSize`): - **Off the grid rectangle (wall death):** The true cell projects mostly off-canvas. Fallback: draw the alarm block at the snake's current head (`snake[0]`/`snake2[0]`, the last valid cell) via the painter's sort, **and** also draw the true OOB coordinate as a sliver on top afterward. - **Within the grid rectangle (self/opponent/head-to-head, or Iron Snake interior void):** Inserted into the painter's sort directly. Correctly shows Iron Snake deaths where the head entered the interior void. The `drawBlockShadow` calls and duplicated `drawBlock` calls from the earlier on-top rendering approach have been removed. Starvation (`deathCells = []`) — this block is a no-op; the freeze shows the last live frame plus the banner only. Death cells are re-read on every `draw()` call, so the highlight persists across window resize and any redraws that occur while frozen. ### Banner DOM and CSS Mirrors the existing `#starvation-warning` pattern: - **`index.html`:** `
` added inside `.canvas-wrap` as a sibling after `#starvation-warning` (~line 97). Overlays the canvas without shifting layout; no `showScreen` call needed. - **`src/style.css`:** `.death-banner` modeled on `.starvation-warning` (lines 184–206) but centered (`top:50%; transform:translate(-50%,-50%)`), larger, alarm-red, `pointer-events:none`, with its own pulse keyframe. Two methods near `updateStarvationWarning` (~line 1750): - `showDeathBanner()` — builds text from `deathReason` (plus per-snake `r1`/`r2` for two-player) and appends " — Press any key", then unhides the element. - `hideDeathBanner()` — re-hides the element. Banner copy: | Reason | Text | |---|---| | `wall` | "Hit the wall!" | | `self` | "Ran into itself!" | | `opponent` | "Crashed into the other snake!" | | `head-to-head` | "Head-on collision!" | | `starved` | "Starved!" | Two-player mode composes with P1/P2 labels. ### Acknowledgement Branch in `handleKeyPress` (after line 752) Inserted at the very top of `handleKeyPress`, after the INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT guard and **before** the Enter/r/Escape branches: ```typescript if (this.awaitingDeathAck) { if (e.repeat || e.key === 'Shift' || e.key === 'Control' || e.key === 'Alt' || e.key === 'Meta') return e.preventDefault() this.awaitingDeathAck = false this.hideDeathBanner() const finalize = this.pendingFinalize this.pendingFinalize = null finalize?.() return } ``` Ordering is critical: this fires before Enter/r/Escape branches, so during the freeze the first key dismisses to Game Over rather than triggering restart or menu. After `finalize()` runs, `showScreen('game-over')` is called and normal Game Over key handling resumes unchanged. `e.repeat` and bare modifiers are ignored to prevent a held key from skipping the freeze instantly. **Camera key fall-through:** Rotate, auto-rotate toggle, overhead, and perspective keys are whitelisted to fall through to their normal handlers during the freeze, turning the frozen board into an inspectable "death cam." All other keys dismiss the freeze. ## Files Modified - **`src/main.ts`** — new fields (~154); resets (~1104, ~1110); `beginDeathFreeze`, `showDeathBanner`, `hideDeathBanner` (~1750/1800); four death sites (1161, 1183, 1231, 1275); death-cell painter's-sort integration in `draw()`; ack branch in `handleKeyPress` (after 752). - **`index.html`** — `#death-banner` div inside `.canvas-wrap` (~line 97). - **`src/style.css`** — `.death-banner` styles and pulse keyframe (modeled on lines 184–206). ## Behavioral Notes - `saveHighScore`, `maybePromptForEntry`, and bvb `addEntry` calls fire at ack-time (key press), not at the death instant. Nothing depends on them firing earlier. Closing the tab during the freeze skips score recording — accepted as an edge case. - Iron Snake, overhead view, and both-snakes-dying-on-the-same-tick (draw / head-to-head) are all handled by the render/bounds logic. - The freeze is purely additive — `endGame` and `endGameTwoPlayer` run verbatim as the finalize step, so all downstream Game Over behavior (high-score name entry, Play Again, leaderboard, Menu) is unchanged. - The death marker sorts correctly relative to the new head and tail graphics (head drawn at 1.6× body height, tail drawn as a tapered wedge) because all are inserted into the same painter's-algorithm list before sorting.
